question: Which statement best describes a DMN Business Knowledge Model (BKM) in Camunda?

options.text: A reusable function containing a piece of decision logic that optionally accepts parameters and returns a single result whose name is the BKM element name.
A connector that synchronously invokes an external decision microservice from a process.
A scheduled timer wrapper that triggers a DMN decision at a fixed interval.
An audit-log entry the engine writes after each decision evaluation.

a.text: Per the docs, a BKM is a reusable function containing decision logic, typically built as a FEEL literal expression. It can optionally accept parameters and returns a single result whose name is the BKM element name.

b.text: A BKM is decision logic that runs inside the decision engine, not a connector that calls an external microservice.

c.text: BKMs are not timer-based wrappers; they are reusable pieces of decision logic invoked from decisions.

d.text: BKMs are part of the decision model, not audit-log entries. They define logic to be evaluated, not records of past evaluations.

explanation: A BKM is a DMN element that encapsulates reusable decision logic — typically a FEEL literal expression — and is invoked by decisions. It optionally takes parameters and produces a single result variable named after the BKM element.
